concerns.text: Tasks T029/T030/T034 implement a fallback to internal DFT validation and explicitly log that FR-006 (External Validation) is unmet. This constitutes a silent relaxation of the spec's mandatory requirement (FR-006) and Constitution Principle VII (Physical Reality Validation). The tasks do not include a step to formally amend the spec or document the scope reduction as a governance action, violating the 'no requirement-weakening' constraint.

Task T022 defines the 'High' potential bin solely on the 4V data point, whereas Spec US-2 and SC-002 mandate measuring shifts between 'low-potential (0-2V) and high-potential (3-5V)' bins. The task silently narrows the acceptance criterion (3-5V range) to a single point (4V) without a corresponding spec amendment or explicit 'staged' justification in the task description, violating the 'no silent constitution drift' constraint.

Task T014 lists 'bond lengths, angles, dihedrals' as the geometric features to extract. Spec FR-003 explicitly requires 'at least 5 geometric features'. The task description does not specify a mechanism to count or select specific instances to guarantee the minimum count of 5 distinct features is met, creating a risk that the deliverable falls short of the FR requirement.
